lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Forty years in Houston heat
Stitching pipe with steady hands
Long days, sweat and steel
You built more than pipe and plans
You built this home for all of us
With callused hands and faith

[Pre-Chorus]
Now the rods are in the truck
And the lake is calling you
Seventy candles shining bright
On a life we made with you

[Chorus]
Happy 70, Bob
We’re singing loud and proud
Happy 70, Bob
You still hold us all around
For the kids, for the years
For the love you gave so true
Happy 70, Bob
We love you

[Verse 2]
Now you drift out on Lake Conroe
Watching morning kiss the line
A cooler, cap, and fishing pole
That gentle grin of yours is mine
You taught our three kids how to stand
With a backbone, kind and strong

[Pre-Chorus]
Every mile and every shift
Led you home where you belong
Now we raise a toast to all you’ve done
And the man you are so long

[Chorus]
Happy 70, Bob
We’re singing loud and proud
Happy 70, Bob
You still hold us all around
For the kids, for the years
For the love you gave so true
Happy 70, Bob
We love you

[Bridge]
Forty years went by like rain
On a tin roof in the spring
Still you’d smile and kiss us goodnight
Like it was the greatest thing
And if the water’s calm this evening
That’s your well-earned view
You gave us all your best days
Now all our best belong to you

[Chorus]
Happy 70, Bob
We’re singing loud and proud
Happy 70, Bob
You still hold us all around
For the kids, for the years
For the love you gave so true
Happy 70, Bob
We love you

[Outro]
Happy 70, Bob
Happy 70, Bob
Our home is full because of you
Happy 70, Bob
title: Bob on Conroe
